content.text: Yes, `mcp__claude_ai_New_Relic__authenticate` is available, but your auth attempts both returned "server reconnection failed." You likely need to restart Claude Code to establish the connection.

hookInfos.command: case "$TERM_PROGRAM" in ghostty) B='com.mitchellh.ghostty';; iTerm.app) B='com.googlecode.iterm2';; WarpTerminal) B='dev.warp.Warp-Stable';; Apple_Terminal) B='com.apple.Terminal';; *) B='';; esac; terminal-notifier -title 'Claude Code' -message 'Claude finished' -sound 'Glass' ${B:+-activate "$B"} 2>/dev/null || true

input.command: cat ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); mcp=d.get('mcpServers',{}); [print(k) for k in mcp]" 2>/dev/null || echo "No settings or no mcpServers"
cat ~/.claude/settings.json | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | grep -A5 -i "newrelic\|new_relic\|new-relic" || echo "not found"
input.description: Check New Relic MCP server config

input.command: cat ~/.claude/settings.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d.get('mcpServers',{}), indent=2))"

input.description: Check installed plugins for New Relic
Check remote settings for MCP config

content.text:
These are **claude.ai-hosted MCP servers** (not local). The auth flow goes through claude.ai OAuth, and the "server reconnection failed" error is a known Claude Code bug where the process doesn't reconnect after OAuth completes.

Options:

1. **Update Claude Code** — `npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` (you're on 2.1.96, check if newer)
2. **Configure New Relic MCP locally** instead of through claude.ai integration — add it directly to `~/.claude/settings.json` under `mcpServers`
3. **File a bug** — this is a known reconnection issue with claude.ai OAuth-based MCP servers

Do you want to set up New Relic MCP locally? If so, what's your New Relic API key and account ID?

hookInfos.command: case "$TERM_PROGRAM" in ghostty) B='com.mitchellh.ghostty';; iTerm.app) B='com.googlecode.iterm2';; WarpTerminal) B='dev.warp.Warp-Stable';; Apple_Terminal) B='com.apple.Terminal';; *) B='';; esac; terminal-notifier -title 'Claude Code' -message 'Claude finished' -sound 'Glass' ${B:+-activate "$B"} 2>/dev/null || true

input.description: Check what env vars newrelic-mcp needs
Check all New Relic env vars

content.text:
Need your New Relic credentials:
1. **User API key** (starts with `NRAK-...`) — from [one.newrelic.com → API keys](one.newrelic.com/api-keys)
2. **Account ID** — visible in the URL when logged into New Relic

Note: this is a **User API key** (not the agent license key in `.env`).
